note: Running catalog / source of truth for the coin-archive site. Photos are the HEIC files in ~/Desktop/COINS (converted to JPG for viewing). lat/lng to be geocoded at build time. locationConfidence: confirmed | inferred | best-guess.

people.Jack: Grandfather; the collector. Signs cards 'J' or 'Jack'.
people.Greg: Jack's grandson; creator of this project.

coins.cardText: J found outside Walgreen's
coins.locationResolved: Walgreens, 2400 N Broadway St, Knoxville, TN 37917
coins.locationNote: True 1999 store unknown (Walgreens was just entering Knoxville ~2000); Greg chose 2400 N Broadway as a plausible for-fun pin.

coins.cardText: Legion Field J 10/15/83 Birmingham T-41 - A-34
coins.locationResolved: Legion Field, Birmingham, AL
coins.locationNote: Real, still-standing landmark — easy geocode.

event.name: Tennessee at Alabama football
event.detail: Upset of 14-point-favorite Alabama; Tennessee won late on Johnnie Jones' 66-yard touchdown run.

coins.familyMemory: Greg attended this game with Jack. (More of Greg's recollections to be added.)

coins.cardText: Jack - found UT Stadium 9/13/86 - UT/Miss St football game. Jack & Adam
coins.locationResolved: Neyland Stadium, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: Real landmark — easy geocode.

event.name: Tennessee vs Mississippi State football
event.detail: Mississippi State upset No. 8 Tennessee at Neyland Stadium; attendance 89,897.

coins.familyMemory: Adam (Jack's grandson, Greg's brother) was at this game with Jack.

coins.cardText: 10/27/72 J White Store Clinton Highway
coins.locationResolved: White Stores, 5400 Clinton Hwy, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: Defunct East-TN grocery chain. 5400 Clinton Hwy location documented in Knoxville city directories (via Groceteria.com) for 1960/1965/1970/1974/1980 — open in 1972. In Jack's own Inskip neighborhood (Clinton Hwy corridor, near his home off Paula Rd).

coins.cardText: J - found Nixon Deli 1/23/98
coins.locationResolved: Nixon's Deli North, 508 Merchants Dr, Knoxville, TN 37912
coins.locationNote: Nixon's Deli (local chain, founded 1976) had several Knoxville locations. Chose the 'North' store at 508 Merchants Dr — by far the closest to Jack's house off Paula Rd in Inskip (Merchants Dr is his neighborhood corridor, same area as the White Store on Clinton Hwy). Assumes that storefront existed in 1998 (plausible, not directly verified).

coins.cardText: March 10, 1985 - Lahaina, Maui - Pioneer Inn. J
coins.locationResolved: Pioneer Inn, 658 Wharf St, Lahaina, Maui, HI 96761
coins.locationNote: Historic 1901 hotel — oldest on Maui, longest continuously operating hotel in Hawaii; beside the famous Lahaina banyan tree on the harbor. Jack's farthest-flung find.

place.name: Pioneer Inn
place.where: Lahaina waterfront, beside the banyan tree, Maui, Hawaii (658 Wharf St)
place.story: The Pioneer Inn opened in 1901 on the Lahaina waterfront — the town's first hotel, and for more than a century the oldest on Maui and the longest continuously operating hotel in Hawaii. A two-story plantation-style building beside the great banyan tree, it kept watch over the harbor for 122 years and hosted everyone from sea captains to movie stars. When Jack found this dime out front in March 1985, the Inn had already stood for 84 years. On August 8, 2023, the wildfire that swept through Lahaina burned it to the ground. The coin outlasted the building.
place.photoCredit: Historic American Buildings Survey, Library of Congress (public domain)

coins.cardText: J. found - 11/2/91 UT/Mem. St game outside stadium
coins.locationResolved: Neyland Stadium, Knoxville, TN

place.name: Neyland Stadium
place.where: Banks of the Tennessee River, University of Tennessee, Knoxville
place.story: Neyland Stadium has stood on the bank of the Tennessee River in Knoxville since 1921, growing from a few thousand seats into one of the largest stadiums in America — a sea of orange that packs in well over 100,000 on game day. Named for the legendary coach Gen. Robert Neyland, it has been the Saturday heartbeat of the city for a century. Jack found coins here more than once.

event.name: Tennessee vs Memphis State football
event.detail: Tennessee routed Memphis State at Neyland; attendance 96,664 (televised on TBS).

coins.cardText: 10/15/85 Jack found lot Super X Clinton Highway
coins.locationRaw: Super X parking lot, Clinton Highway
coins.locationResolved: SupeRx drugstore, Clinton Highway, Knoxville, TN (exact address not pinned)
coins.locationNote: Found in the parking lot ('found lot'). Exact Clinton Hwy address not pinned; placed on Clinton Hwy as a home-corridor best guess (same road as the White Store at 5400 Clinton Hwy). Pin-able via a 1985 city directory if ever wanted.

place.name: Super X (SupeRx)
place.where: Clinton Highway, North Knoxville (Jack's home corridor)
place.story: Super X — more properly SupeRx — was the Kroger company's drugstore chain, launched in 1961 and grown to hundreds of stores across the South and Midwest. When Jack found this penny in its Clinton Highway parking lot in October 1985, the chain was in its final stretch under Kroger: within a year Kroger sold the SupeRx stores off, and by 1994 the name was gone entirely, absorbed into Revco (later CVS). It was an ordinary neighborhood drugstore a short drive from Jack's house — and like the White Store down the road, it has vanished.

coins.cardText: 5/24/86 parking lot K-Mart - Clinton Hwy
coins.cardNote: No finder initial written on this card; attributed to Jack (the collector) by default.
coins.locationResolved: Kmart #3065, Clinton Plaza Shopping Center, 5094 Clinton Hwy, Knoxville, TN 37912
coins.locationNote: Confirmed: Kmart #3065 at Clinton Plaza, 5094 Clinton Hwy — opened Nov 29, 1973 (replaced a smaller Kmart in the same plaza, so effectively the one Clinton Hwy Kmart), closed 2003. Memorial Day weekend find. A few blocks from the White Store (5400 Clinton Hwy) — tight home cluster.

place.name: Kmart (Clinton Plaza)
place.where: Clinton Plaza Shopping Center, 5094 Clinton Highway, North Knoxville
place.story: The Clinton Highway Kmart — store #3065 — anchored the Clinton Plaza Shopping Center at 5094 Clinton Highway. The big store Jack knew opened on November 29, 1973, replacing a smaller Kmart in the same plaza, and it ran for three decades before closing in 2003. For a North Knoxville family it was the everything-store: garden hoses, records, blue-light specials. Jack found this penny in its parking lot on the Saturday of Memorial Day weekend, 1986.

coins.cardText: 9/20/88 Jack found Las Vegas casino floor
coins.locationResolved: Las Vegas Strip (specific casino unknown)
coins.locationNote: Specific casino unknown (per Greg) — pin dropped in the middle of the Las Vegas Strip on purpose.

place.name: The Las Vegas Strip
place.story: Somewhere on the Las Vegas Strip, on a casino floor, Jack spotted a quarter on the carpet and pocketed it. There's no telling which casino, so this pin sits in the middle of the Strip. September 1988 caught Las Vegas at the tail end of its old self: the megaresort era was about to begin — The Mirage would open just over a year later, in November 1989, and remake the Strip for good. The Vegas Jack walked was still the older neon-and-lounge city of the era just before.

coins.cardText: 10/2/92 Chris found Shoney's lot Nashville
coins.locationResolved: Shoney's, Nashville, TN (specific location unknown)
coins.locationNote: Found by Chris (Jack's wife) in a Shoney's parking lot; specific Nashville Shoney's unknown — pin placed in Nashville. First coin found by Chris, and first by someone other than Jack/grandkids.

place.name: Shoney's
place.story: Chris — Jack's wife — found this penny in the parking lot of a Shoney's in Nashville. Shoney's, the Southern family restaurant chain headquartered in Nashville, was a road-trip and Sunday-lunch fixture across the South for decades.

coins.cardText: 9/27/70 J McDonald's lot Broadway
coins.locationResolved: McDonald's, Broadway, Knoxville, TN (specific location unknown)
coins.locationNote: Found in the parking lot. McDonald's on Broadway, North Knoxville (near Jack's home area); specific early-1970 location not pinned. OLDEST found-date in the collection so far (1970).

coins.cardText: 1/29/99 Chris found front McDonald's Valdosta, GA
coins.locationResolved: McDonald's, Valdosta, GA (pin on Valdosta per Greg)
coins.locationNote: Found by Chris (Jack's wife) in front of a McDonald's in Valdosta, GA. Per Greg: don't find exact address — just pin Valdosta. NEW STATE (Georgia) = 5th state.

coins.cardText: 7/6/73 J Mail Box Paula Dr
coins.locationResolved: Jack's home — the mailbox, Paula Drive, Fountain City, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: FIRST of the Paula Drive HOME SERIES — coins Jack found around his own house (this one at the mailbox). Many more coming; ALL reuse this fixed home coordinate (36.0312, -83.9486, Paula Dr off Cedar Lane, Fountain City) so they stack on one home pin. First NICKEL in the collection.

coins.cardText: J found Proffitt's, Bra Dept. 5/31/00
coins.locationResolved: Proffitt's (Bra Department), West Town Mall, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: Jack found it in the BRA DEPARTMENT at Proffitt's — Greg believes the West Town Mall store (West Knoxville). Proffitt's = Knoxville-grown department store, folded into Belk mid-2000s (gone). NEWEST found-date so far (2000) — extends collection range to 1970–2000.

place.name: Proffitt's
place.story: Jack found this penny in — of all places — the bra department at Proffitt's. Proffitt's was a Knoxville institution: a department store that grew from a small-town Tennessee shop into a regional chain and an anchor at West Town Mall. The Proffitt's name is gone now, folded into Belk in the mid-2000s. The bra-department detail, though, is forever.

coins.cardText: 10/20/87 Chris found Viet Nam Memorial Washington, DC
coins.locationResolved: Vietnam Veterans Memorial, National Mall, Washington, DC
coins.locationNote: Found by Chris (Jack's wife) at the Vietnam Veterans Memorial, DC. A 1987 penny found in 1987 = brand new (0 years circulating). NOTE: DC is not a state — will affect the 'across N states' stat wording; flag for Greg.

place.name: Vietnam Veterans Memorial
place.story: This 1987 penny — brand new that year — came home from the Vietnam Veterans Memorial in Washington, D.C. Maya Lin's black granite wall, dedicated in 1982, carries the names of more than 58,000 Americans lost in the war, and visitors have always left things along it — flowers, letters, coins pressed into the seams. Chris brought one back.

coins.cardText: 11/4/85 Chris found McDonald's Trenton, GA
coins.locationResolved: McDonald's, Trenton, GA
coins.locationNote: Found by Chris at a McDonald's in Trenton, GA (NW Georgia near Chattanooga). FIRST MULTI-COIN CARD — two coins taped together: 1974 penny + 1981 nickel (face value 6¢). Site display will need to handle two coins on one card (denomination 'penny + nickel'; value 6¢). 2nd Georgia find.

coins.cardText: 6/11/87 Chris found in room Bavarian Haus Motel Frankenmuth, MI
coins.locationRaw: Bavarian Haus Motel (in room), Frankenmuth, MI
coins.locationResolved: Bavarian Haus Motel, Frankenmuth, MI
coins.locationNote: Found by Chris IN THE ROOM at the Bavarian Haus Motel, Frankenmuth, MI ('Little Bavaria'). NEW STATE = Michigan. A real, distinct motel (NOT the big Bavarian Inn Lodge, which opened 1986); appears on vintage 1970s Frankenmuth postcards. Place photo TBD — vintage postcard sources noted.

place.name: Bavarian Haus Motel
place.story: Chris found this dime right in the room at the Bavarian Haus Motel in Frankenmuth — Michigan's 'Little Bavaria,' a whole town done up in Alpine timber and gingerbread trim for the tourists who came for chicken dinners and Christmas shops. The motel was one of many that put them up, and it still turns up on Frankenmuth postcards from the 1970s.

coins.cardText: Jack found 8/21/96 parking lot of Office Depot
coins.locationResolved: Office Depot, 7111 Kingston Pike, Knoxville, TN 37919
coins.locationNote: Exact address given: Office Depot, 7111 Kingston Pike, Knoxville TN 37919 (West Knoxville / Bearden). Found in the parking lot. Brand-new 1996 penny found in 1996 (0 yrs circulating).

coins.cardText: 5/4/96 Jack found Coke machine Best Western Charlotte, NC
coins.locationRaw: Coke machine, Best Western, Charlotte, NC
coins.locationResolved: Best Western (Coke machine), Charlotte, NC
coins.locationNote: Found by JACK (not Chris — so Jack traveled too) at the Coke machine in a Best Western, Charlotte, NC. NEW STATE = North Carolina. Specific Best Western unknown — pin on Charlotte.

coins.cardText: 12/10/00 Chris found in couch front room
coins.locationResolved: Jack's home — in the couch, front room, Paula Drive, Fountain City, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: HOME SERIES — Chris found this in the couch in the front room at home on Paula Drive. 1980 penny found in 2000 = 20 years circulating (one of the longest). Reuses the home coordinate; stacks on the home pin.

coins.cardText: 12/6/96 J found parking lot K-Mart Clinton Hwy
coins.locationResolved: Kmart #3065, Clinton Plaza, 5094 Clinton Hwy, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: SECOND Clinton Highway Kmart coin (groups with coin-009). 1979 penny found 1996 = 17 yrs circulating. placeKey=kmart-clinton for the location-grouping model Greg wants.

coins.cardText: 1/31/03 C found at E. Towne Mall
coins.locationResolved: East Towne Mall (Knoxville Center), 3001 Knoxville Center Drive, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: Signed 'C' = CHRIS (card-initial convention: J=Jack, C=Chris). East Towne Mall, East Knoxville (3001 Knoxville Center Drive, by I-640). Opened 1984; officially 'Knoxville Center' by 2003 but locals kept saying East Towne. CLOSED Jan 31, 2020 — exactly 17 yrs to the day after this find — demolished 2021 = GONE. NEWEST found-date so far (2003) -> range now 1970–2003.

place.name: East Towne Mall
place.story: East Towne Mall opened on Knoxville's east side in 1984 and was, for a couple of decades, the place — anchor stores, holiday crowds, food-court lunches. By 2003 the signs out front read 'Knoxville Center,' but everyone still called it East Towne — and so did Chris. The mall faded over the years, closed for good on January 31, 2020, and was torn down in 2021. Chris found this penny there on January 31, 2003 — seventeen years to the day before it shut its doors.

coins.cardText: 7/31/96 Jack found at slot machine in Horseshoe Casino Tunica
coins.locationResolved: Horseshoe Casino, Tunica, MS
coins.locationNote: Found by Jack at a SLOT MACHINE in the Horseshoe Casino, Tunica, MS. NEW STATE = Mississippi. Second quarter in the collection; second casino find (cf. coin-010 Vegas).

place.name: Horseshoe Casino, Tunica
place.story: Jack found this quarter at a slot machine in the Horseshoe Casino in Tunica, Mississippi — the boomtown that turned a stretch of Delta cotton country into the South's casino capital in the 1990s. A quarter, left at a slot machine, where quarters are supposed to disappear. Jack pocketed it instead.

coins.cardText: 3/13/76 J Safe Deposit Vault B of K (Bank of Knoxville) Merchant Rd
coins.locationRaw: safe deposit vault, Bank of Knoxville, Merchant Rd
coins.locationResolved: Bank of Knoxville, Merchants Drive, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: Found by Jack IN THE SAFE DEPOSIT VAULT at Bank of Knoxville on Merchants Drive (North Knoxville, near home — same corridor as Nixon's Deli). 1968 penny found 1976 = 8 yrs. The bank has likely since merged/closed; pin on Merchants Dr.

coins.cardText: 10/24/97 C found at Smithsonian History
coins.locationResolved: Smithsonian National Museum of American History, Washington, DC
coins.locationNote: Signed 'C' = Chris. Smithsonian National Museum of American History, National Mall, DC. Second DC find — Chris found both (also the Vietnam Memorial, coin-016). Penny 1987 found 1997 = 10 yrs.

place.name: Smithsonian — National Museum of American History
place.story: Found at the Smithsonian's National Museum of American History on the Mall in Washington — the nation's attic, free to all, where the Star-Spangled Banner and the keepsakes of a whole country are kept. Chris found a penny among them, and added it to a keepsake of her own.

coins.cardText: J found 2/23/96 Best Western Jellico
coins.locationResolved: Best Western, Jellico, TN
coins.locationNote: Found by Jack at the Best Western in Jellico, TN (small town on I-75 at the KY line, Campbell County). DIFFERENT Best Western from coin-020 (Charlotte) — separate placeKey. 1974 penny found 1996 = 22 yrs circulating (longest in the collection so far).

coins.cardText: 10/17/87 Jack found Fredersburg, VA Hampton Motel lot
coins.cardNote: Card spells it 'Fredersburg' — Jack's typo for Fredericksburg; display the correct spelling.
coins.locationResolved: Hampton Motel parking lot, Fredericksburg, VA
coins.locationNote: Found by JACK at the Hampton Motel parking lot, Fredericksburg, VA. NEW STATE = Virginia. TRIP CONNECTION: 3 days before coin-016 (Chris, Vietnam Memorial DC, 10/20/87) — looks like a shared Jack+Chris Oct-1987 East-Coast trip, each finding a coin (Jack in VA, Chris in DC). Could surface as a 'trip' thread later.

coins.cardText: J found Office Depot lot 9/17/01
coins.locationResolved: Office Depot, 7111 Kingston Pike, Knoxville, TN 37919
coins.locationNote: SECOND Office Depot coin — SAME location as coin-019 (7111 Kingston Pike); groups with it. placeKey=office-depot-kingston (assign to coin-019 too in the refactor). 2000 penny found 2001 = 1 yr. Date 9/17/2001 = six days after 9/11 — quiet historical texture (an ordinary errand the week the country reeled).

coins.cardText: 3/21/97 J found front K-Mart (Broadway)
coins.locationResolved: Kmart, 4434 N Broadway (Northgate Plaza), Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: DIFFERENT Kmart from the Clinton Hwy one (coin-009/022). This is the BROADWAY / Fountain City Kmart at 4434 N Broadway (Northgate Plaza). Opened 1963, CLOSED Aug 2016 (became a peddler's mart) = gone. placeKey=kmart-broadway. 1993 penny found 1997 = 4 yrs.

place.name: Kmart (Broadway / Northgate Plaza)
place.story: The other Knoxville Kmart — the Northgate Plaza store at 4434 North Broadway, up in Fountain City. It opened in 1963 with an auto center and gas pumps out front, and ran for nearly 53 years before closing in 2016, when the building became a flea market. Jack found this penny out front in 1997, when the big red K was still lit.

coins.cardText: J found car wash 2/26/00 Cedar Lane
coins.locationResolved: Car wash, Cedar Lane (near the railroad tracks / Merchants), Fountain City, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: Found by Jack at a car wash on Cedar Lane (Greg: near the railroad tracks at Merchants Dr) — Fountain City / North Knoxville, near home. 1996 penny found 2000 = 4 yrs.

coins.cardText: 5/4/96 Chris found at rest stop I-85 South Carolina
coins.locationResolved: I-85 rest stop, South Carolina
coins.locationNote: Found by Chris at an I-85 rest stop in South Carolina. NEW STATE = South Carolina. Exact stop unknown (Greg: educated guess) — pinned at the I-85 SC Welcome Center (Fair Play, MM 0, GA line) as a best guess; could be any I-85 SC rest area (others: Anderson MM 17/24, Blacksburg MM 103 near NC line). TRIP CONNECTION: SAME DAY (5/4/96) as coin-020 (Jack, Best Western Charlotte NC). I-85 runs to Charlotte, so this looks like a Jack+Chris road trip up I-85 to Charlotte — Chris found this at the rest stop, Jack found one at the Charlotte motel that night.

coins.cardText: 9/30/92 Jack found Woodlands, Texas
coins.locationResolved: The Woodlands, TX
coins.locationNote: Jack found TWO pennies (1974 + 1976) in The Woodlands, TX (suburb N of Houston). NEW STATE = Texas. Second multi-coin card (cf. coin-017, penny+nickel). Face value 2¢; years circulating 18 / 16.

coins.cardText: Found 2/14/84 J yard 5608 Paula
coins.locationResolved: Jack's home — in the yard, 5608 Paula Drive, Fountain City, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: HOME SERIES — Jack found this in the YARD at home on Valentine's Day 1984. Card reveals the HOME ADDRESS: 5608 Paula Drive → at next publish, update PLACES.home.where to '5608 Paula Drive, Fountain City'. 1971 penny found 1984 = 13 yrs.

coins.cardText: 5/24/90 Jack found Ingles parking lot
coins.locationResolved: Ingles, Cedar Lane, Fountain City, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: Found by Jack in the parking lot of the Ingles on CEDAR LANE, Fountain City (RESOLVED: coin-038 confirms 'Ingles lot Cedar Lane'; #35 and #38 group as placeKey 'ingles'). 1988 penny found 1990 = 2 yrs.

coins.cardText: J found Burger King 9/2/97 phone booth (Merchant Rd)
coins.say: J · found Burger King · 9/2/97 · phone booth (Merchant Rd)

coins.locationResolved: Burger King, Merchants Drive, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: Found by Jack IN A PHONE BOOTH at the Burger King on Merchants Drive (North Knoxville, near home). Quarter 1989 found 1997 = 8 yrs. Lovely vintage detail (phone booth, 1997).

coins.cardText: J found car wash 8/1/92
coins.locationResolved: Car wash, Cedar Lane, Fountain City, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: Same Cedar Lane car wash as coin-031 (groups, placeKey car-wash-cedar-lane). 1978 penny found 1992 = 14 yrs.

coins.say: 12/10/98 · J · found Ingles lot · Cedar Lane

coins.locationNote: Ingles on Cedar Lane (same as coin-035; groups, placeKey ingles). Confirms the Ingles location = Cedar Lane. 1977 penny found 1998 = 21 yrs (one of the longest-circulated).

coins.locationResolved: Arby's, Broadway, North Knoxville (Fountain City area)
coins.locationNote: FIRST three-coin card — Jack found 3 pennies (1971, 1971, 1985) at the Arby's on Broadway. Value 3¢; years circulating 21 / 21 / 7. placeKey arbys-broadway.

coins.locationResolved: Gary's house, 1704 Waterview Trail, Knoxville, TN

coins.cardText: 2/2/84 J driveway 5608 Paula
coins.locationResolved: Jack's home — in the driveway, 5608 Paula Drive, Fountain City, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: HOME SERIES — Jack found this in the DRIVEWAY at home (5608 Paula). A 1950 WHEAT penny — OLDEST mint year in the whole collection, and at 34 yrs circulating (1950->1984) the LONGEST-traveled. Same Feb 1984 as coin-034 (yard, 2/14/84).

coins.cardText: 2-18-86 Chris Arby's - Broadway Rest Room
coins.locationResolved: Arby's on Broadway, North Knoxville, TN — in the rest room
coins.locationNote: SECOND Arby's-Broadway coin (with coin-039). Card-confirmed finder = CHRIS (not Jack). 1980 penny found 1986 = 6 yrs.

coins.cardText: 9/19/88 Jack found Las Vegas at Slots
coins.say: 9/19/88 · Jack · found Las Vegas · at the slots
coins.locationResolved: Las Vegas, NV — at the slot machines (exact casino unknown)
coins.locationNote: SECOND Vegas-Strip coin (with coin-010, 9/20/88) — this one a day EARLIER, 9/19/88, at the slots. 1984 quarter found 1988 = 4 yrs. Card middle digit a touch ambiguous (could read 9/9) but Greg confirms 9/19/88.

coins.cardText: 7/12/80 J Winn Dixie Lot Clinton Highway
coins.locationResolved: Winn-Dixie parking lot, Clinton Highway, North Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: TWO pennies, both 1976 (multi-coin card). NEW PLACE needed at publish: winn-dixie-clinton — Winn-Dixie on Clinton Highway, same North-Knoxville corridor as White Store / Super X / Kmart-Clinton. Winn-Dixie exited the Knoxville/TN market years ago, so this store is almost certainly GONE → status 'gone'. Pin ~36.0415/-83.9855, best-guess on Clinton Hwy (Greg can correct). Found 7/12/80 (4 yrs).

coins.cardText: Chris found Gary driveway 3/3/91
coins.locationResolved: Gary's house — in the driveway, 1704 Waterview Trail, West Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: SECOND gary-house coin (with coin-040, the trash penny). THREE pennies — 1979, 1986, 1989 — found by CHRIS in Gary's driveway, 3/3/91. (Gary = Greg's dad.) Multi-coin card.

coins.cardText: Jack found 12/16/92 outside Dr. Hargrove office
coins.locationResolved: Dr. (Raymond L.) Hargrove's office, 140 Dameron Ave, North Knoxville, TN 37917
coins.locationNote: Dr. Raymond L. Hargrove, MD — North Knoxville internist/gastroenterologist (in practice since 1961). Office 140 Dameron Ave, Knoxville 37917 (Old North Knox, near downtown / Fort Sanders-St. Mary's). Census-geocoded 35.9785/-83.9276. CONFIRMED by Greg (2026-06-20) as the right Dr. Hargrove. NEW PLACE at publish: hargrove-office (status standing).

coins.cardText: 8/15/71 Chris Found A & P Clinton Highway
coins.locationResolved: A&P grocery, Clinton Highway, North Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: NEW PLACE at publish: a-and-p-clinton — A&P (Great Atlantic & Pacific Tea Co.) grocery on Clinton Highway. A&P is long defunct → status 'gone'. Same Clinton Hwy corridor as White Store / Super X / Kmart / Winn-Dixie. Exact storefront unknown — best-guess pin ~36.042/-83.985. Finder = CHRIS (card). 1967 dime found 1971 = 4 yrs.

coins.cardText: 10/10/92 Jack Found outside Stud. Ctr. UT-Ark game
coins.say: 10/10/92 · Jack · outside the Student Center · UT–Arkansas game
coins.locationResolved: University of Tennessee student center, Knoxville, TN — UT–Arkansas game day
coins.locationNote: NEW PLACE at publish: ut-student-center — the University of Tennessee student center on campus, near Neyland. Jack found this on game day, 10/10/92. status standing.

event.name: Tennessee vs Arkansas football
event.detail: Oct 10, 1992 at Neyland Stadium. Arkansas — 1-4, interim coach, freshman QB — upset No. 4 Tennessee on Todd Wright's 41-yard field goal with 2 seconds left, after the Vols led 24-16 late.

coins.cardText: Chris Found Rapid City, S.D. 6/9/84
coins.locationResolved: Rapid City, South Dakota
coins.coinNote: Canadian dime (1979) — the FIRST foreign coin in the collection (Canada 10¢, Bluenose schooner).
coins.locationNote: NEW PLACE at publish: rapid-city-sd — Rapid City, South Dakota (gateway to the Black Hills / Mount Rushmore). FIRST South Dakota pin AND first FOREIGN coin: a 1979 CANADIAN dime. Finder = CHRIS. Pin = Rapid City center 44.0805/-103.2310. PUBLISH TODO: flag as foreign in the UI; decide whether the Canadian 10¢ counts toward the US$ total (probably note separately). 1979 found 1984 = 5 yrs.

coins.cardText: Jack 11/7/87 UT-Louisville game in stadium
coins.say: 11/7/87 · Jack · UT–Louisville game · in the stadium
coins.locationResolved: Neyland Stadium, Knoxville, TN — UT vs Louisville, 11/7/87
coins.locationNote: FOURTH Neyland coin. The 11/7/87 UT-Louisville game was at NEYLAND STADIUM, KNOXVILLE (box score Louisville at Tennessee, attendance 92,084), Tennessee won 41-10, on a Saturday. Greg did NOT attend. (His separate Thursday-at-Louisville/Muhammad-Ali memory is a different game with no coin in the collection — dropped, do not pursue.)

event.name: Tennessee vs Louisville football
event.detail: Nov 7, 1987 at Neyland Stadium, Knoxville; attendance 92,084.

coins.cardText: Found 3/17/79 Raking front yard. J
coins.locationResolved: Jack home — front yard while raking, 5608 Paula Drive, Fountain City, Knoxville, TN
coins.microSpot: in the front yard, while raking
coins.locationNote: HOME SERIES — FOUR nickels (20c) Jack found raking the front yard at home, 3/17/79. Nickels taped face-DOWN (Monticello side up) so mint years are NOT visible/unknown. Multi-coin card.

coins.cardText: Jack found Cumberland Ave 12/2/89 - TN/Vandy game
coins.say: 12/2/89 · Jack · Cumberland Ave · after the TN–Vandy game
coins.locationResolved: Cumberland Avenue (the Strip), Knoxville, TN — after the TN–Vanderbilt game
coins.locationNote: NEW PLACE at publish: cumberland-ave — Cumberland Avenue (the Strip) along the UT campus edge, Knoxville. Jack found FOUR 1989 pennies here after the 12/2/89 TN-Vandy game. status standing. Multi-coin card. Same campus/game-day flavor as coin-048 (student center, 1992 Ark game) but a distinct place.

event.name: Tennessee vs Vanderbilt football
event.detail: Dec 2, 1989 at Neyland Stadium, Knoxville. No. 8 Tennessee won its regular-season finale; the Vols finished 11-1 and beat Arkansas in the Cotton Bowl.

coins.cardText: 6/20/87 Jack found Yellowstone Park Grant Village
coins.locationResolved: Grant Village, Yellowstone National Park, Wyoming

POSSIBLE 1987 TRIP CLUSTER: coin-019 (Bavarian Haus, Frankenmuth MI) was Chris on 6/11/87; this is Jack in WY on 6/20/87 — maybe legs of a big summer-1987 trip. Flag for the timeline trip-bracket.

coins.locationResolved: Kmart, 7428 Kingston Pike, West Knoxville, TN — out front
coins.familyMemory: Jack was buying tapes here to send to Gary — Greg's dad — who was deployed in Australia during the Vietnam War. The penny he picked up out front, in October 1969, is the earliest find in the whole collection.
coins.locationNote: NEW PLACE at publish: kmart-kingston (status GONE — the Kingston Pike Kmart closed in the Sears/Kmart collapse ~2018). Census-geocoded 7428 Kingston Pike = 35.92953/-84.03290 (West Knox, Bearden). EARLIEST FIND in the collection now: 10/4/1969, predates the 9/27/1970 McDonald's — when published, 'first find' becomes 1969 and the span grows to 37 yrs (oldest by MINT year is still the 1950 penny). FAMILY MEMORY: Jack bought tapes here to send to Gary (Greg's dad), deployed in Australia during the Vietnam War — render as 'From the family'. 3rd Knoxville Kmart (with kmart-clinton, kmart-broadway).

coins.cardText: Jan-1986 Chris Found $20.00 Lion Country Safari Palm Beach, Fla
coins.say: Jan 1986 · Chris · found $20 · Lion Country Safari
coins.locationRaw: Lion Country Safari, Palm Beach, FL
coins.locationResolved: Lion Country Safari, Loxahatchee (West Palm Beach), Florida
coins.locationNote: NON-COIN FIND (kind:bill): Chris found a $20 BILL (not a coin; the bill isn't kept, only the slip survives) at Lion Country Safari, the first cageless drive-through safari park in the US (opened 1967, still operating west of West Palm Beach, FL), Jan 7 1986. Slip only says 'Jan 1986'; Greg supplied the 7th. PLAN at publish: show it as a special find on the timeline + map (slip as the image) marked '$20 bill — the only paper money in the box', give it a facts card ('the biggest find'), but KEEP it OUT of the coins count and the coin $ total (so 'N coins · $3.07' stays about coins). OPEN Q for Greg: keep $20 separate vs fold into headline total. NEW PLACE lion-country-safari (FL, standing). Pin ~26.7038/-80.2386.

coins.cardText: MS Noordam Jack Casino 2/8/90
coins.say: 2/8/90 · Jack · in the casino · MS Noordam
coins.locationResolved: Aboard the MS Noordam (Holland America), at St. Thomas, U.S. Virgin Islands — in the ship's casino
coins.locationNote: RESOLVED (research agent + primary source). FIRST at-sea/cruise find. Jack found a 1966 quarter in the CASINO of the MS NOORDAM (1984 Holland America 'Noordam III') on Thu 2/8/1990. CONFIRMED via the St. Petersburg Times Cruise Guide of 2/18/1990: that winter the Noordam ran a 7-day EASTERN Caribbean loop round-trip from PORT EVERGLADES (Fort Lauderdale), every Saturday, ports San Juan / Tortola / St. Thomas / Nassau. Feb 8 = Day 6 of the cruise that embarked Sat Feb 3 1990 → in port, most likely ST. THOMAS (USVI), Tortola the alt — almost certainly NOT a sea day. Pin placed at St. Thomas (Charlotte Amalie 18.3419/-64.9307). STATS: state=null, country='at sea' so NOT a US state; at publish generalize usStates to filter (c.state && !c.country). Ask Greg if family recalls which island that day (would settle St. Thomas vs Tortola).
coins.familyMemory: Jack and Chris were on this cruise together. The day Jack found the quarter in the casino, the Noordam was calling at St. Thomas, in the U.S. Virgin Islands.

cruise.itinerary: 7-day Eastern Caribbean, round-trip Port Everglades (Fort Lauderdale)
cruise.ports: San Juan, Tortola, St. Thomas, Nassau
cruise.onThisDay: Day 6 (Thu Feb 8) — in port, most likely St. Thomas (or Tortola)
cruise.source: St. Petersburg Times Cruise Guide, Feb 18, 1990 (primary, 10 days after)

coins.cardText: 11/21/95 Jack Found Outside Dr. Muse Office Bldg.
coins.locationResolved: Dr. (William S.) Muse's office, Newland Professional Building, 2001 Laurel Ave, Knoxville, TN — outside the building
coins.locationNote: RESEARCH RESULT (subagent): the only Knoxville 'Dr. Muse' whose career spans 1991 is Dr. WILLIAM S. MUSE JR., MD — a UROLOGIST in the Fort Sanders medical district (UT med school '67, practicing since the mid-70s; affil. Fort Sanders Regional + UT Medical Center). His office building = the NEWLAND PROFESSIONAL BUILDING, 2001 Laurel Ave (a 7-story medical office tower behind Fort Sanders Regional, BUILT 1979 so it existed in 1991) — matches the card's 'Office Bldg' wording. Census-geocoded 35.95747/-83.93814 — literally next to Fort Sanders Hospital (coin-086). CONFIDENCE = strong inference (identity + building), NOT confirmed from a 1991 source (would need the 1991 Polk's city directory). Caveat: Jack lived in Fountain City near St. Mary's, but no Muse practice found on the north side. No dentist named Muse exists. To confirm: did Jack see a urologist? Jack, a 1991 nickel outside the bldg, 11/21/95 (4 yrs).

coins.cardText: Jack 1/16/91 Front Knoxville Kawasaki
coins.locationResolved: Knoxville Kawasaki, Clinton Highway (~5000-5500 block), North Knoxville, TN — out front
coins.locationNote: RESEARCH RESULT (subagent): 'Knoxville Kawasaki' has NO surviving online address record. But firsthand recollections (VolNation 'gone but not forgotten Knoxville retailers' thread) consistently place a Clinton Highway Kawasaki dealer of that era in the ~5000-5500 block of Clinton Hwy, North Knoxville — 'in a converted house in front of Kmart' and 'just south of Merchant Drive'. That Kmart = the 5094 Clinton Hwy Kmart (kmart-clinton, coins 009/022) — so Knoxville Kawasaki sat right among Jack's Clinton Hwy haunts. Pin moved into that block ~36.0035/-83.9790. CONFIDENCE = strong for the corridor, weak for exact storefront (none found; would need the offline 1990-91 Polk's directory at the McClung Collection). status GONE (defunct/no records). Separate 'Law's Kawasaki' existed on Alcoa Hwy (S. Knox) — different shop, probably not this one. Jack, a 1987 dime out front, 1/16/91.

(Card year digit slightly ambiguous; using Greg's 9/18/90.)

coins.locationNote: NEW PLACE at publish: lottery-store-broadway — a store that sold lottery tickets on Broadway, N Knoxville (TN lottery began 2004). Jack, a 1980 penny, in the lot, 10/24/07. NO store name/address on the card and Greg doesn't know which — NOT researchable (generic). Best-guess pin on N Broadway ~36.010/-83.925. status standing. THE NEWEST FIND in the whole collection now (10/24/2007) — extends the span to 1969-2007 = 38 yrs (was ...-2006); the 'first find->last' fact updates to 2007. 1980 penny found 2007 = 27 yrs.

coins.cardText: C Found Gary's 8/20/93
coins.locationResolved: Gary's house, 1704 Waterview Trail, West Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: FIFTH gary-house coin (with 040 trash, 045 driveway, 069 fireplace, 077 yard) — and ALL FIVE were found by CHRIS. Chris, a 1992 dime, at Gary's, 8/20/93 (1 yr). Gary = Greg's dad.

coins.cardText: J Found Cordele, GA Gas Station 1/29/94
coins.locationResolved: Gas station, Cordele, Georgia
coins.locationNote: NEW PLACE at publish: gas-station-cordele — a gas station in Cordele, GA (an I-75 town in south-central Georgia, the 'Watermelon Capital', a classic Florida-route stop). Jack, FOUR pennies (1992,1994,1994,1972), 1/29/94. Multi-coin card. status standing. Best-guess Cordele pin 31.9635/-83.7824 (no address). GA already a state.

coins.cardText: 9/25/98 Chris Found o/s Ingles Cedar Ln

coins.locationNote: SEVENTH Ingles coin (with 035, 038, 059, 066, 084, 089) — Cedar Lane Ingles near home, the collection's single biggest non-home hub. Chris, two pennies (1995, 1974), outside the store, 9/25/98. Multi-coin card. THIS IS THE 100TH COIN in the collection.
